title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: Making HTTP requests to URLs derived from user input allows attackers to probe internal network services, cloud metadata endpoints, and bypass firewall controls (OWASP A10:2021 / CWE-918).

finding.message: Potential SSRF via user-controlled URL at '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Validate user-supplied URLs against an allowlist of permitted domains/IP ranges.
Block requests to RFC 1918 addresses (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x) and cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254).
Use a dedicated HTTP client library that supports SSRF protection (e.g., SafeCurl).
